_description: User proxy preference store — three-tier (universal, domain, project). See user_proxy_design.json for semantics.

CRITICAL_SECURITY.description: Security, data access, credential handling, or irreversible-operation preferences. Require 2 explicit user confirmations before promotion from tentative to active. CANNOT be auto-learned from implicit behavior (implicit_acceptance, no_signal). Only explicit_approval signals count.
CRITICAL_SECURITY.promotion_gate: Two independent explicit_approval signals from distinct sessions, separated by at least 24 hours.

HIGH_STAKES.description: Architecture decisions, multi-file refactors, cross-module changes, or new dependency introductions. Require 1 explicit confirmation. Auto-learning disabled during warmup period (first 50 decisions).
HIGH_STAKES.promotion_gate: Single explicit_approval signal, or 3+ consistent implicit_acceptance signals across distinct contexts (post-warmup).

STANDARD.description: Style conventions, naming patterns, single-file formatting, or cosmetic changes. Single implicit signal sufficient for promotion. Fully auto-learnable.
STANDARD.promotion_gate: Single explicit_approval, or 1+ implicit_acceptance with no corrections within 14 days.

universal.scope: Style invariants across all projects — applied before project/domain tiers.

entries.description: Python code follows PEP 8 naming: snake_case for variables/functions, PascalCase for classes
entries.rule: All Python identifiers MUST follow PEP 8 — snake_case for functions and variables, PascalCase for classes, UPPER_SNAKE for constants.

entries.description: Git commits use English conventional commit format: feat:/fix:/chore:/docs: prefix + short description
entries.rule: Every commit message MUST use conventional commit format in English: type(scope): description. Types: feat, fix, chore, docs, refactor, test.

entries.description: Every code change MUST run full test suite before commit — no exceptions
entries.rule: Before every commit that changes .py files, run: python -m pytest tests/ -x -q -m 'not slow'. Must pass 100%.

entries.description: Proxy Agent auto-executes technical decisions (code quality, architecture) but defers product/feature direction to user
entries.rule: Decisions classified as TECHNICAL (code structure, tool choice, performance optimization) may be auto-executed. Decisions classified as PRODUCT (feature scope, UX behavior, business logic) MUST be escalated to user.

entries.description: Conservative process (backup first, precise changes, test thoroughly) but root-cause-focused solutions — fix the problem, not the symptom
entries.rule: Before modifying critical modules (gateway, pipeline core, shadow state): (1) backup existing file to .claude/backups/, (2) minimal precise edit targeting root cause, (3) run related tests before commit. Fix the underlying cause, not the surface symptom.

entries.description: Only write documentation for major features or architecture changes — routine fixes documented via commit messages
entries.rule: Create docs files (specs, plans, retrospectives) only for: new features >100 lines, architecture changes, API/schema changes. Routine bug fixes and tweaks are documented via commit messages only.

domain.scope: Per-domain patterns (Python project conventions, TypeScript/react patterns, database/sql conventions).

project.scope: Project-specific overrides. Highest priority — supersedes universal and domain tiers for conflicting preferences.

_entry_schema.tag: enum — one of CRITICAL_SECURITY, HIGH_STAKES, STANDARD
_entry_schema.description: string — human-readable description of the preference
_entry_schema.rule: string — machine-actionable rule (e.g. 'function names MUST start with a verb')
_entry_schema.confidence: float 0.0-1.0 — calibrated confidence in this preference
_entry_schema.signals: list of signal records: [{type, timestamp, context_fingerprint, session_id}]
_entry_schema.confirmations_received: int — number of explicit user confirmations (for CRITICAL_SECURITY gate)
_entry_schema.supersedes: string or null — id of preference this one replaces
